strategy: Field a team of 4: a hitter with high damage and pierce, a 'Minion Killa' with 21%+ pierce and a 4-pip AoE, and supports with trained elemental/spirit blades. The Rat resists 15% to most schools but NOT Fire or Ice, so a Fire or Ice hitter is ideal.

Win by Round 4. The Titan clock deals ~1,043 shadow AoE on Rounds 2 and 5 (escalating on 10/15/20/25) and wipes the team with 1,000,000 damage at Round 30. Minion summons also take three turns to ramp, so a fast kill outruns everything.

Do not trigger summons carelessly. Traps summon a Death minion (Titan 07), heals summon a healer minion (Titan 34), and blades or globals summon a Storm minion (Titan 291). Front-load blades on the hitter and save traps/Feint for the burst round.

R1: everyone blades the hitter (unless the dispeller must cast Melt). R2: the Minion Killa fires an Unstoppable-enchanted 4-pip AoE to clear minions while others keep blading; trap the Rat if you have it.

R3: everyone blades the hitter, aiming for 10-11 total buffs (trained/sharpened/item school blades plus tri-blades). R4: apply Feint and any last trap, the healer casts Rebirth or Unicorn if needed, then the hitter fires the kill shot.
Feint carefully. The first Feint is only a warning, but a second single-target Feint bounces all debuffs back onto the caster. Use Mass Feint instead - it never triggers the bounce.
Cast Melt to remove the Rat's 7-pip group Frostbite that stuns the whole team. Have the hitter port home and Dungeon Recall to enter last in turn order.
cheatNotes: Wizards who join after the battle starts are hit with +60% Fire/Ice/Global traps and a Shadow Dimension Shift that dumps all of the Rat's debuffs onto them, so never join late.
The first Feint placed on him is free - he only shouts a warning and lets it stick.
A second single-target Feint triggers a Shadow Dimension Shift that strips every debuff off him and slaps it onto the wizard who cast it, so only Feint him once with single-target Feint.
Mass Feint does not trigger the debuff-bounce, making it the safe way to stack Feints on him.
The Balance version of Feint is not removed but instead summons a Titan Experiment 07 minion via his trap-summon cheat.
While all three enemy slots are full he cannot summon new minions, so leaving a low-threat minion alive can actually block worse summons until a slot opens.
Casting any trap makes him summon a Titan Experiment 07 (1 HP, ~103% resist to normal schools), a Death minion that ramps over three turns.
You can dispel the pending Titan 07 with Strangle to stop the summon entirely.
On its first turn the 07 casts Plague (-25%) and hardens to 10 HP / 105% resist.
On its second turn it casts Mass Infection and hardens to 100 HP / 107% resist, so it is much tougher if left alive two rounds.
On its third turn it casts a multi-target Bad Juju (2 pip) on the whole team and then self-destructs.
Mass Feint also triggers the Titan 07 summon, so weigh its use against spawning a minion.
Mockenspiel likewise triggers the Titan 07 summon.
Any spell that places a negative charm triggers the Titan 07 summon, not just traps.
Casting any heal makes him summon a Titan Experiment 34, a Life/healer minion that will keep him alive if left up.
On its first turn the 34 casts Guiding Light and Sanctuary, then hardens to 10 HP / 105% resist.
On its second turn it casts Regenerate (225+1,100) if the Rat is hurt, or passes if he is full, then hardens to 100 HP / 107% resist.
On its third turn it casts Rebirth (730) if the Rat is hurt, or Spinysaur on a random wizard if he is full, then self-destructs.
If the 34 used Rebirth, the Rat follows with Doom and Gloom (-75%), gutting your own healing for a while.
Rebirth on a defeated minion revives it for one more round, and it repeats the exact spell it cast before dying.
Pet-cast heal spells also trigger the Titan 34 summon, so watch may-cast heal pets.
Link and Power Link do not count as heals and will not trigger the Titan 34 summon.
Casting any blade or global makes him summon a Titan Experiment 291, a Storm minion that throws heavy bolts.
You can dispel the pending Titan 291 with Dissipate to stop the summon.
On its first turn the 291 casts Windstorm (+25%) and hardens to 10 HP / 105% resist.
On its second turn it casts Wild Bolt (up to 1,150) on a random wizard and hardens to 100 HP / 107% resist.
On its third turn it casts a multi-target Insane Bolt (1,125) on the whole team and self-destructs.
He shouts a flavor line whenever a Titan 291 self-destructs, no mechanical effect.
On Rounds 2, 10, and 20 the Titan hits the Sky Anchor for ~1,043 shadow damage to all wizards, escalating on the later rounds.
On Rounds 5, 15, and 25 the Titan deals ~1,043 shadow damage to all wizards, escalating on the later rounds.
At the start of Round 30 (and every round after) the Titan deals 1,000,000 shadow damage with high pierce, wiping the team, so you must win well before Round 30.
